metadata.title: Clinical Data Needed to Support the Licensure of Seasonal Inactivated Influenza Vaccines

rules.rule_text: A BLA for a new seasonal inactivated influenza vaccine must demonstrate effectiveness against influenza illness through an adequate and well-controlled clinical study.
rules.source_section: III.A — Traditional Approval of a BLA for a New Seasonal Inactivated Influenza Vaccine; III.A.1 Effectiveness
rules.verbatim_quote: demonstration of effectiveness against influenza illness in an adequate and well-controlled clinical study would support licensure of a new seasonal inactivated influenza vaccine.
rules.exceptions: Accelerated approval pathway available as alternative (see Section III.B); waiver under 21 CFR 601.25(d)(2) if alternative method adequately substantiates effectiveness.

rules.rule_text: The clinical endpoint efficacy study must assess influenza illness as the primary endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: In this document, a clinical endpoint efficacy study refers to a clinical trial in which influenza illness is assessed as the primary endpoint.

rules.rule_text: The study population must be carefully considered. A placebo-controlled clinical efficacy study in a population not at increased risk for complications from influenza allows estimation of absolute clinical effectiveness against influenza illness.
rules.verbatim_quote: A placebo-controlled clinical efficacy study conducted in a population that is not at increased risk for complications from influenza would allow for a precise estimation of clinical effectiveness against influenza illness (absolute efficacy).
rules.exceptions: Alternatively, a population at increased risk may be studied using adequate sample size to demonstrate non-inferiority to a U.S. licensed product with regard to clinical effectiveness.

rules.rule_text: The case definition for influenza illness must be prospectively defined; culture confirmation, viral typing, and antigenic characterization should be included to increase specificity.
rules.verbatim_quote: The case definition for influenza illness should be prospectively defined. Inclusion of culture confirmation, viral typing and antigenic characterization in the case definition increases the specificity.

rules.rule_text: Study sample size calculations must be based on estimates of vaccine effectiveness and influenza attack rates. The study must be powered to assess the lower bound of the two-sided 95% CI of vaccine effectiveness, anticipated to be substantially above zero (e.g., in the range of 40 to 45%).
rules.verbatim_quote: Study sample size calculations should be based on estimates of vaccine effectiveness and influenza attack rates. The study should be powered to assess the lower bound of the two-sided 95% confidence interval (CI) of vaccine effectiveness, anticipated to be substantially above zero (e.g., in the range of 40 to 45%).

rules.rule_text: Immunogenicity evaluations must be conducted in a substantial number of study participants as important elements of the study design to characterize the immune response post-vaccination.
rules.verbatim_quote: Immunogenicity evaluations in a substantial number of study participants are important elements of the study design. Characterization of the immune response elicited post-vaccination in the clinical endpoint efficacy study may allow for extrapolating the effectiveness to other populations if they have an immune response to vaccination comparable to that observed in the clinical endpoint efficacy study.

rules.rule_text: For populations not included in the clinical efficacy study (e.g., children 6 to 59 months and those ≥65 years), immunogenicity bridging studies may be conducted. Co-primary endpoints must include: 1) geometric mean titer (GMT), and 2) seroconversion rates for hemagglutination inhibition (HI) antibody to each viral strain.
rules.verbatim_quote: Studies should be adequately powered to assess the following co-primary endpoints for each of these viral strains: 1) geometric mean titer (GMT), and 2) rates of seroconversion, defined as the percentage of subjects with either a pre-vaccination HI titer < 1:10 and a post-vaccination HI titer ≥ 1:40 or a pre-vaccination HI titer ≥ 1:10 and a minimum four-fold rise in post-vaccination HI antibody titer.

rules.rule_text: Alternatively, non-inferiority immunogenicity studies comparing a new vaccine to a U.S. licensed seasonal vaccine may support use in populations not in the clinical endpoint efficacy study, using co-primary endpoints of GMT and seroconversion rates for HI antibodies to each viral strain.
rules.verbatim_quote: non-inferiority immunogenicity studies comparing a new vaccine to a U.S. licensed seasonal vaccine may support the use of the new vaccine in populations not included in the clinical endpoint efficacy study. This is true when the comparator vaccine is indicated for use in the population under study and when the comparator vaccine has clinical effectiveness data.
rules.exceptions: Comparator must have clinical effectiveness data and be indicated for the population under study; not applicable if comparator was only accelerated-approved with clinical benefit awaiting confirmation.

rules.rule_text: Safety of the new vaccine must be well characterized in pre-licensure clinical trials. Local and systemic reactogenicity events must be well defined in all age groups for whom approval is sought, with appropriate grading scales in the study protocol.
rules.verbatim_quote: The safety of the new vaccine should be well characterized in pre-licensure clinical trials. Local and systemic reactogenicity events should be well defined in all age groups for whom approval of the vaccine is sought. Appropriate grading scales to describe the severity of the adverse events should be included in the study protocol.

rules.rule_text: Serious adverse events must be monitored and collected for all subjects throughout the duration of studies, per 21 CFR 312.23, 312.32, 312.56, 312.60, and 312.62.
rules.verbatim_quote: Serious adverse events must be monitored and collected for all subjects throughout the duration of the studies (21 CFR 312.23, 312.32, 312.56, 312.60 and 312.62).

rules.rule_text: The study protocol must include a clinic visit or telephone contact at least six months post-vaccination to ascertain additional serious adverse events and new onset of chronic illnesses.
rules.verbatim_quote: The protocol should include a clinic visit or telephone contact at least six months post-vaccination to ascertain additional serious adverse events and new onset of chronic illnesses that may have occurred in the interim.

rules.rule_text: For vaccines using novel manufacturing processes and/or adjuvants, laboratory safety tests including hematologic and clinical chemistry evaluations may be needed pre- and post-vaccination in the first clinical studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: For vaccines using novel manufacturing processes and/or adjuvants, laboratory safety tests including hematologic and clinical chemistry evaluations, may be needed pre-and post-vaccination in the first clinical studies.
rules.exceptions: Only triggered when novel manufacturing processes or adjuvants are used.

rules.rule_text: The sponsor must assess the safety of the investigational vaccine in several thousand subjects who receive the product in controlled clinical trials, prior to licensure. Early dialogue with CBER is required to agree on the size of the safety database.
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that you assess the safety of your investigational vaccine in several thousand subjects who receive the product in the controlled clinical trials described above. You are encouraged to initiate an early dialogue with CBER to agree on the size of the safety database needed to support product licensure.
rules.exceptions: Accelerated approval may have a smaller safety database requirement (see Section III.B.2).

rules.rule_text: The timing of pediatric clinical development and the size of the pediatric safety database must be discussed with CBER.
rules.verbatim_quote: The timing of the clinical development and the size of the safety database to support use in the pediatric age groups warrants discussion with CBER.
rules.exceptions: PREA deferral may apply; see Section III.C.4.

rules.rule_text: Accelerated approval for a seasonal inactivated influenza vaccine must be based on adequate and well-controlled clinical trials establishing an effect on a surrogate endpoint (HI antibody response) reasonably likely to predict clinical benefit.
rules.source_section: III.B — Accelerated Approval of a BLA
rules.verbatim_quote: Such an approval will be based on adequate and well-controlled clinical trials establishing that the biological product has an effect on a surrogate endpoint that is reasonably likely, based on epidemiologic, therapeutic, pathophysiologic, or other evidence, to predict clinical benefit (21 CFR 601.41).
rules.exceptions: Available when influenza vaccine shortage exists for U.S. market at time of new vaccine approval.

rules.rule_text: Under accelerated approval, the sponsor must commit to conduct confirmatory postmarketing studies of clinical effectiveness in preventing influenza during the next influenza season. Postmarketing studies must be adequate and well-controlled and conducted with due diligence.
rules.verbatim_quote: Approval under this section will be subject to the requirement that the sponsor study the biological product further, to verify and describe its clinical benefit, where there is uncertainty as to the relation of the surrogate endpoint to clinical benefit (21 CFR 601.41). Postmarketing studies must also be adequate and well-controlled and should be conducted with due diligence (21 CFR 601.41).
rules.exceptions: Marketing approval may be withdrawn if postmarketing study fails to verify clinical benefit or sponsor fails to perform with due diligence (21 CFR 601.43(a)(1) and (2)).

rules.rule_text: The BLA for accelerated approval must include results from one or more well-controlled studies designed to meet immunogenicity endpoints, and a commitment to conduct confirmatory postmarketing studies. Protocols for postmarketing studies must be submitted with the original BLA.
rules.verbatim_quote: To be considered for accelerated approval, a BLA for a new seasonal inactivated influenza vaccine should include results from one or more well-controlled studies designed to meet immunogenicity endpoints and a commitment to conduct confirmatory postmarketing studies of clinical effectiveness in preventing influenza during the next influenza season. The protocols for these studies should be submitted with the original BLA.

rules.rule_text: For accelerated approval via non-inferiority immunogenicity trial: the upper bound of the two-sided 95% CI on the ratio of the GMTs (GMTreference/GMTnew) must not exceed 1.5 for each viral strain.
rules.verbatim_quote: The upper bound of the two-sided 95% CI on the ratio of the GMTs (GMTU.S. licensed vaccine/GMTnew vaccine) should not exceed 1.5.
rules.exceptions: A proposal for use of a different GMT ratio should be based upon the characteristics of the assay that will be used to assess antibody responses.

rules.rule_text: For accelerated approval via non-inferiority immunogenicity trial: the upper bound of the two-sided 95% CI on the difference in seroconversion rates (Seroconversionreference − Seroconversionnew) must not exceed 10 percentage points for each viral strain.
rules.verbatim_quote: The upper bound of the two-sided 95% CI on the difference between the seroconversion rates (Seroconversion U.S. licensed vaccine - Seroconversionnew vaccine) should not exceed 10 percentage points.

rules.rule_text: For accelerated approval via placebo-controlled immunogenicity trial: the study must be adequately powered to assess co-primary endpoints for HI antibodies to each viral strain: 1) seroconversion rates, and 2) percentage of subjects achieving HI antibody titer ≥ 1:40.
rules.verbatim_quote: Alternatively, a placebo-controlled immunogenicity trial in which HI antibody responses to the new vaccine are assessed may be supportive of accelerated approval if the study is adequately powered to assess the co-primary endpoints for HI antibodies to each viral strain contained in the vaccine: 1) seroconversion rates, and 2) percentage of subjects achieving an HI antibody titer ≥ 1:40.
rules.exceptions: A saline placebo is acceptable only if the population is not routinely recommended for annual seasonal influenza vaccination by the ACIP or if the study is conducted off-season.

rules.rule_text: For adults < 65 years and pediatric populations under accelerated approval (placebo-controlled study): the lower bound of the two-sided 95% CI for seroconversion must meet or exceed 40%.
rules.verbatim_quote: For adults < 65 years of age and for the pediatric population: The lower bound of the two-sided 95% CI for the percent of subjects achieving seroconversion for HI antibody should meet or exceed 40%.

rules.rule_text: For adults < 65 years and pediatric populations under accelerated approval: the lower bound of the two-sided 95% CI for the percent of subjects achieving HI antibody titer ≥ 1:40 must meet or exceed 70%.
rules.source_section: III.B.1.b — Effectiveness criteria, adults < 65 years, titer threshold
rules.verbatim_quote: The lower bound of the two-sided 95% CI for the percent of subjects achieving an HI antibody titer ≥ 1:40 should meet or exceed 70%.

rules.rule_text: For adults ≥ 65 years under accelerated approval: the lower bound of the two-sided 95% CI for seroconversion must meet or exceed 30%.
rules.verbatim_quote: For adults ≥ 65 years of age: The lower bound of the two-sided 95% CI for the percent of subjects achieving seroconversion for HI antibody should meet or exceed 30%.

rules.rule_text: For adults ≥ 65 years under accelerated approval: the lower bound of the two-sided 95% CI for the percent of subjects achieving HI antibody titer ≥ 1:40 must meet or exceed 60%.
rules.source_section: III.B.1.b — Effectiveness criteria, adults ≥ 65 years, titer threshold
rules.verbatim_quote: The lower bound of the two-sided 95% CI for the percent of subjects achieving an HI antibody titer ≥ 1:40 should meet or exceed 60%.

rules.rule_text: Safety data for accelerated approval must be collected from all enrolled subjects in pre-licensure clinical trials per 21 CFR 312.23, 312.32, 312.56, 312.60, and 312.62, following the safety evaluation outline in Section III.A.3.
rules.verbatim_quote: Safety data must be collected from subjects enrolled in pre-licensure clinical trials intended to support the accelerated approval of a new seasonal inactivated influenza vaccine (21 CFR 312.23, 312.32, 312.56, 312.60 and 312.62).

rules.rule_text: For accelerated approval with adequate marketing experience using the same manufacturing process: a safety database of at least 1,150 vaccine recipients may be sufficient to rule out a serious adverse event rate of 1 in 300 (upper limit of two-sided 95% CI = 0.0032).
rules.verbatim_quote: A total safety database large enough to rule out a serious adverse event that occurs at a rate of 1 in 300 may be sufficient when a sponsor has adequate marketing and safety experience with the same manufacturing process for a seasonal vaccine licensed outside the United States... the upper limit of the two-sided 95% CI of the true serious adverse event rate is 0.0032 (<1 in 300) when no serious adverse event is observed among 1150 subjects who received vaccine in clinical trials.
rules.exceptions: For vaccines using novel manufacturing processes (e.g., cell-culture) or novel adjuvants, the required safety database size will be larger and must be discussed with CBER.

rules.rule_text: Postmarketing confirmatory studies for accelerated approval must follow the design for effectiveness studies described in Section III.A.1 (traditional approval effectiveness data).
rules.verbatim_quote: For the design of postmarketing studies the sponsor should refer to studies described in Section III.A.1. on effectiveness data to support traditional approval of new seasonal inactivated influenza vaccines.

rules.rule_text: Clinical lot consistency must be demonstrated by showing that three consecutively manufactured, final formulated bulk lots of vaccine elicit equivalent immune responses. The 95% CI on GMT ratio for each viral strain in pair-wise comparison must be entirely within 0.67 and 1.5.
rules.verbatim_quote: The objective of a clinical lot consistency study is to show consistency of manufacturing and performance of the final product by demonstrating that three consecutively manufactured final formulated bulk lots of vaccine elicit equivalent immune responses... We recommend a pair-wise comparison of the 95% CI on the ratio of GMTs for each viral strain contained in the three vaccine lots as an appropriate primary endpoint. The two-sided 95% CI on the GMT ratio should be entirely within 0.67 and 1.5.
rules.exceptions: Lot consistency may be evaluated and incorporated into postmarketing commitment studies on a case-by-case basis.

rules.rule_text: For adjuvanted seasonal inactivated influenza vaccines, data supporting safety of the adjuvanted formulation and added benefit over the unadjuvanted formulation must be submitted in the BLA.
rules.source_section: III.C.3 — Adjuvanted Seasonal Inactivated Influenza Vaccines
rules.verbatim_quote: Data supporting the safety of the adjuvanted formulation and added benefit over the unadjuvanted formulation must be submitted in the BLA (42 U.S.C. 262(a)(2)(C)(i); 21 CFR 601.2).

rules.rule_text: For adjuvanted vaccines — dose and formulation selection: a comparative clinical study of adjuvanted vs. non-adjuvanted vaccines (same antigen amount) must demonstrate that immune response elicited by the adjuvanted antigen is better than unadjuvanted antigen alone.
rules.source_section: III.C.3 — Adjuvanted Vaccines, Dose and Formulation Selection
rules.verbatim_quote: For initial dose and formulation selection, a comparative clinical study of adjuvanted vs. non-adjuvanted vaccines that both contain the same amount of antigen should demonstrate that the immune response elicited by the adjuvanted antigen is better than that elicited by the same antigen alone.
rules.exceptions: In a shortage setting, adjuvant use may be supported by data from a comparative study demonstrating non-inferiority immune responses elicited by the adjuvanted vaccine containing a lower amount of antigen.

rules.rule_text: When adding an adjuvant to a licensed seasonal influenza vaccine without antigen sparing, the immune response elicited by the adjuvanted formulation must be substantially better than that elicited by the unadjuvanted vaccine. Meaningful differences in HI antibody titer and seroconversion rates must be specified and justified.
rules.source_section: III.C.3 — Adding an Adjuvant to a Licensed Seasonal Influenza Vaccine
rules.verbatim_quote: If an adjuvant is added to a licensed seasonal influenza vaccine for use without antigen sparing effects (i.e., the dose of the antigen is not changed), the immune response elicited by the adjuvanted vaccine formulation should be substantially better than that elicited by the unadjuvanted vaccine for the study population... meaningful differences in HI antibody titer and seroconversion rates between adjuvanted and unadjuvanted formulations should be specified and justified.
rules.exceptions: For accelerated approval, meaningful differences in immunogenicity are required; for confirmatory postmarketing studies, meaningful differences in clinical endpoints must be specified and justified.

rules.rule_text: All BLA submissions (or clinical efficacy supplements) for a new active ingredient, new indication, new dosage form, new dosing regimen, or new route of administration must contain a pediatric assessment under PREA unless the sponsor has obtained a waiver or deferral from FDA.
rules.verbatim_quote: Under PREA, all applications (or clinical efficacy supplements) submitted under section 505 of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (the Act) (21 U.S.C. 355) or section 351 of the Public Health Service Act (PHS Act) (42 U.S.C. 262) for a new active ingredient, new indication, new dosage form, new dosing regimen, or new route of administration are to contain a pediatric assessment (pediatric clinical data) unless the sponsor has obtained a waiver or deferral from FDA (21 U.S.C. 355c).
rules.exceptions: Waiver or deferral from FDA under 21 U.S.C. 355c.

rules.rule_text: A pharmacovigilance plan must be included in the BLA submission, especially if the vaccine involves novel manufacturing processes and/or novel adjuvants. The format must comply with ICH E2E guidance.
rules.verbatim_quote: As part of your BLA submission, you should include a pharmacovigilance plan, especially if the vaccine involves novel manufacturing processes and/or novel adjuvants. The format of these submissions should be in accordance with the International Conference on Harmonisation of Technical Requirements for Registration of Pharmaceuticals for Human Use (ICH E2E guidance).

rules.rule_text: Routine pharmacovigilance must include submission of adverse event reports to the Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System (VAERS) in accordance with 21 CFR Part 600, Subpart D.
rules.verbatim_quote: routine pharmacovigilance should include submission of adverse event reports to the Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System in accordance with 21 CFR Part 600, Subpart D.
rules.exceptions: Expedited reporting may be requested for some adverse events (e.g., serious labeled adverse events) and dose distribution data.

rules.rule_text: BLAs are approved only upon showing that the product is 'safe, pure, and potent' and that the manufacturing facility meets standards ensuring the biological product continues to be safe, pure, and potent. 'Potency' has long been interpreted to include effectiveness (21 CFR 600.3(s)).
rules.verbatim_quote: BLAs are approved only upon a showing that the product is 'safe, pure and potent,' and that the manufacturing facility meets standards designed to assure that the biological product 'continues to be safe, pure, and potent.' In previously issued guidance... FDA stated, 'Potency has long been interpreted to include effectiveness (21 CFR 600.3(s)).'
